title: Restore current-head external review availability
goal: Make independent current-head PR review fail predictably and recoverably when Claude credentials, Codex quota or local read-only sandbox support are unavailable, without weakening merge gates or treating failed model starts as review evidence.

acceptance.assertion: External review adapters report credential, quota and sandbox readiness before consuming a review request; an unavailable provider produces typed blocked evidence rather than a partial review claim.
A successful review is bound to the exact PR head and diff hash, while stale-head reviews are classified as historical findings only.
The workflow defines an allowed independent fallback or an explicit stop condition without converting a self-review or failed model launch into external PASS evidence.
PR review automation reads added files only after the pull-request head is checked out, avoiding base-branch file-hash warnings and ambiguous context.
Focused tests cover missing Claude authentication, exhausted Codex quota, unsupported read-only sandbox, stale review head and successful current-head evidence.

metadata.source: Cabinet PR #107 current-head review closeout on 2026-07-10
observed_failures: Claude GitHub action run 29110821959 failed before model execution because no supported Anthropic authentication input was available.
Local Codex review session 019f4d11-3950-7591-8e77-18f06e51c413 failed first on read-only bubblewrap network setup and then on exhausted usage quota.
The GitHub Codex review remained bound to an obsolete PR head and did not provide current-head evidence.
metadata.cancellation_reason: Grabowski PR #146 replaced mandatory external review availability with risk-scaled, diff-bound self-review. Current gate logic treats external reviews as optional diagnostics, so restoring Claude/Codex availability is no longer a merge-safety prerequisite.
rationale: Current tools/pr_review_gate.py sets external_review_required=false for every review tier and requires one to five self-review iterations according to risk.
Missing or invalid optional external-review evidence produces warnings rather than a merge block.
Credential, quota and provider-specific recovery work would add maintenance and secret-handling surface without restoring a required capability.
A future provider-preflight task should be registered only after repeated operational demand demonstrates value for optional diagnostics.
